text: Orbit subscriptions renew automatically on the same calendar day each month or year, depending on your plan. Your renewal date is shown under Settings > Billing. We email an invoice the moment a renewal is charged.

If you see two charges for the same renewal, it is almost always a temporary authorization hold that drops off in 3-5 business days, not a real duplicate charge. If both charges have settled (left 'pending'), it is a genuine duplicate and we refund the extra charge in full.

Refunds for duplicate charges or billing errors are processed in full and posted back to the original payment method within 5-7 business days. Pro-rated refunds for downgrades are issued as account credit, not cash.

New subscriptions can be refunded within 14 days of the first charge, no questions asked. After 14 days, refunds are granted only for duplicate charges, failed-service incidents, or documented billing errors.

Orbit accepts Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and ACH bank transfer on annual Business plans. We do not accept PayPal or cryptocurrency. Card details are stored with our PCI-compliant processor, never on Orbit servers.

When a renewal payment fails, we retry the card three times over six days and email you each time. The account stays active during the retry window. If all retries fail, the workspace is downgraded to the free tier, not deleted.
Invoices and receipts are available under Settings > Billing > Invoices. Owners and admins can download PDF copies and add a VAT/tax ID that appears on every future invoice.

Orbit has three plans: Free (up to 3 members, 1 workspace), Pro ($12 per member/month, unlimited workspaces, integrations), and Business ($22 per member/month, SSO, audit log, priority support). Annual billing saves two months.

Upgrades take effect immediately and are pro-rated for the current period. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing period so you keep paid features until then. Changing seats mid-cycle is pro-rated automatically.

You can cancel anytime under Settings > Billing > Cancel Plan. Cancellation stops the next renewal; your workspace stays on the paid plan until the end of the period you already paid for, then moves to Free. Data is retained for 90 days after downgrade.

Owners and admins add or remove members under Settings > Members. Removed members free their seat immediately and the unused portion is credited to your next invoice. Guests do not count toward your seat total.

To recover an account, use 'Forgot password' on the sign-in page. Reset links expire after one hour. If you no longer have access to the account email, an Owner must transfer access, or contact support with proof of ownership.

Change your login email under Settings > Profile > Email. A confirmation link is sent to the new address; the change only completes after you click it. The old address can be used to sign in until then.

Workspace ownership can be transferred under Settings > Members by promoting another member to Owner, then demoting yourself. There can be multiple admins but the billing Owner is the contact for invoices and refunds.

Deleting your account is permanent and removes all workspaces you solely own after a 30-day grace period. Export your data first under Settings > Data > Export. Deletion does not auto-issue a refund; request one separately before deleting.

Single sign-on (SAML and Google Workspace) is available on the Business plan. An admin configures it under Settings > Security > SSO. Once enforced, members must sign in through your identity provider; personal passwords are disabled.

Two-factor authentication is available on all plans and can be enforced workspace-wide on Business. Members set it up under Settings > Security > Two-Factor. Lost-device recovery uses one-time backup codes generated at setup.

Owners can export all workspace data (projects, tasks, comments, files) as a ZIP under Settings > Data > Export. Large workspaces are emailed a download link when the export is ready, usually within 30 minutes.
The audit log (Business plan) records sign-ins, permission changes, billing events, and data exports for the past 12 months. Admins can filter by member and event type and export the log as CSV.
Orbit is SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant. Data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256). We never sell customer data and process deletion requests within 30 days.

Pro and Business plans include integrations with Slack, GitHub, Google Calendar, and Zapier. Connect them under Settings > Integrations. Each integration can be scoped to specific workspaces by an admin.
Orbit has iOS and Android apps with offline mode: changes made offline sync automatically when you reconnect. The mobile app supports the same workspaces and permissions as the web app.

Email support is available 24/7 with a 4-hour first-response target. Business-plan customers get priority support with a 1-hour target and access to live chat during business hours (9am-6pm PT, Mon-Fri).

Billing disputes, suspected fraud, account-takeover reports, and anything requiring a refund over $500 are escalated to a human specialist. The specialist can issue refunds, reverse charges, and override automated holds, which the AI assistant cannot do.
